description: The bones of civilizations that came before, slowly being reclaimed by the world they once tried to shape.
biomes: Vines split the cobblestones and trees grow through rooftops. The streets are quiet now, but the buildings remember when they were not.
Stone columns rising from dark water, half-submerged and draped in algae. The carvings are worn but the scale still inspires awe.
Shattered spires jutting from the earth like broken teeth. Rubble surrounds them and the wind whistles through the gaps.
A fortress torn apart by siege or time. The walls lean at wrong angles and the courtyard is open to the sky.
A massive span of stone collapsed into the gorge below. The pieces are enormous, and crossing requires climbing through the wreckage.
Long stretches of ancient stonework, cracked and leaning. Whatever they once enclosed is open ground now, but the outline remains.
Paved stone cutting straight through wilderness, still level after centuries. The trees crowd the edges but cannot reclaim the path.
